title: Add lstocchi as Podman Reviewer
body:
Luca remarkable knowledge of the Windows virtualization platform would be beneficial for reviewing PRs and triaging issues.

His contributions to the Podman repository during the last six months:
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28535
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27932
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27931
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27885
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27650
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26277
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26201
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20478

@lstocchi as per the https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md you need to add a comment to this PR indicating your willingness to assume the responsibilities of a Reviewer.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: tmt tests failed for commit 9762b6b8dd340c1fc939c6131f8537c22f43a3e1. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
Thank you! It would be an honor to become a reviewer for this project. I am happy to accept the role and help you on the Windows side!!

LGTM

Per governance, two maintainers concurring is enough to approve. I'm going to merge.

Process wise it would be nice if we could at least cc all maintainers on such PRs. Not that I have any objections but it is nice to know (officially) when we onboard new members.

cc @containers/podman-maintainers 

---

Welcome @lstocchi, our main developer chat is on `#podman-dev:matrix.org` so I recommend you join that to know what is going in general.
